NKJV.1: A Prayer of David. Bow down Your ear, O LORD, hear me; For I am poor and needy.
NKJV.2: Preserve my life, for I am holy; You are my God; Save Your servant who trusts in You!
NKJV.3: Be merciful to me, O Lord, For I cry to You all day long.
NKJV.4: Rejoice the soul of Your servant,  For to You, O Lord, I lift up my soul.
NKJV.5: For You, Lord, are good, and ready to forgive, And abundant in mercy to all those who call upon You.
NKJV.6: Give ear, O LORD, to my prayer; And attend to the voice of my supplications.
NKJV.7: In the day of my trouble I will call upon You, For You will answer me.
NKJV.8: Among the gods there is none like You, O Lord; Nor are there any works like Your works.
NKJV.9: All nations whom You have made Shall come and worship before You, O Lord, And shall glorify Your name.
NKJV.10: For You are great, and do wondrous things; You alone are God.
NKJV.11: Teach me Your way, O LORD; I will walk in Your truth; Unite my heart to fear Your name.
NKJV.12: I will praise You, O Lord my God, with all my heart, And I will glorify Your name forevermore.
NKJV.13: For great is Your mercy toward me, And You have delivered my soul from the depths of Sheol.
NKJV.14: O God, the proud have risen against me, And a mob of violent men have sought my life, And have not set You before them.
NKJV.15: But You, O Lord, are a God full of compassion, and gracious, Longsuffering and abundant in mercy and truth.
NKJV.16: Oh, turn to me, and have mercy on me! Give Your strength to Your servant, And save the son of Your maidservant.
NKJV.17: Show me a sign for good, That those who hate me may see it and be ashamed, Because You, LORD, have helped me and comforted me.

KJV.1: A Prayer<S>8605</S> of David<S>1732</S>. Bow down<S>5186</S> thine ear<S>241</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, hear<S>6030</S> me: for I am poor<S>6041</S> and needy<S>34</S>. <sup>A Prayer: or, A Prayer, being a Psalm of David</sup>

KJV.12: I will praise<S>3034</S> thee, O Lord<S>136</S> my God<S>430</S>, with all my heart<S>3824</S>: and I will glorify<S>3513</S> thy name<S>8034</S> for evermore<S>5769</S>.
KJV.13: For great<S>1419</S> is thy mercy<S>2617</S> toward me: and thou hast delivered<S>5337</S> my soul<S>5315</S> from the lowest<S>8482</S> hell<S>7585</S>. <sup>hell: or, grave</sup>
KJV.14: O God<S>430</S>, the proud<S>2086</S> are risen<S>6965</S> against me, and the assemblies<S>5712</S> of violent<S>6184</S> men have sought<S>1245</S> after my soul<S>5315</S>; and have not set<S>7760</S> thee before them. <sup>violent: Heb. terrible</sup>
KJV.15: But thou, O Lord<S>136</S>, art a God<S>410</S> full of compassion<S>7349</S>, and gracious<S>2587</S>, longsuffering<S>750</S> <S>639</S>, and plenteous<S>7227</S> in mercy<S>2617</S> and truth<S>571</S>.
KJV.16: O turn<S>6437</S> unto me, and have mercy<S>2603</S> upon me; give<S>5414</S> thy strength<S>5797</S> unto thy servant<S>5650</S>, and save<S>3467</S> the son<S>1121</S> of thine handmaid<S>519</S>.

ESV.1: Incline your ear, O LORD, and answer me, for I am poor and needy.
ESV.2: Preserve my life, for I am godly; save your servant, who trusts in you — you are my God.
ESV.3: Be gracious to me, O Lord, for to you do I cry all the day.
ESV.4: Gladden the soul of your servant, for to you, O Lord, do I lift up my soul.
ESV.5: For you, O Lord, are good and forgiving, abounding in steadfast love to all who call upon you.
ESV.6: Give ear, O LORD, to my prayer; listen to my plea for grace.
ESV.7: In the day of my trouble I call upon you, for you answer me.
ESV.8: There is none like you among the gods, O Lord, nor are there any works like yours.
ESV.9: All the nations you have made shall come and worship before you, O Lord, and shall glorify your name.
ESV.10: For you are great and do wondrous things; you alone are God.
ESV.11: Teach me your way, O LORD, that I may walk in your truth; unite my heart to fear your name.
ESV.12: I give thanks to you, O Lord my God, with my whole heart, and I will glorify your name forever.
ESV.13: For great is your steadfast love toward me; you have delivered my soul from the depths of Sheol.
ESV.14: O God, insolent men have risen up against me; a band of ruthless men seeks my life, and they do not set you before them.
ESV.15: But you, O Lord, are a God merciful and gracious, slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love and faithfulness.
ESV.16: Turn to me and be gracious to me; give your strength to your servant, and save the son of your maidservant.
ESV.17: Show me a sign of your favor, that those who hate me may see and be put to shame because you, LORD, have helped me and comforted me.

NASB.1: A Prayer of David. Incline Your ear, O LORD, [and] answer me; For I am afflicted and needy.
NASB.2: Preserve my soul, for I am a godly man; O You my God, save Your servant who trusts in You.
NASB.3: Be gracious to me, O Lord, For to You I cry all day long.
NASB.4: Make glad the soul of Your servant, For to You, O Lord, I lift up my soul.
NASB.5: For You, Lord, are good, and ready to forgive, And abundant in lovingkindness to all who call upon You.
NASB.6: Give ear, O LORD, to my prayer; And give heed to the voice of my supplications!
NASB.7: In the day of my trouble I shall call upon You, For You will answer me.
NASB.8: There is no one like You among the gods, O Lord, Nor are there any works like Yours.
NASB.9: All nations whom You have made shall come and worship before You, O Lord, And they shall glorify Your name.
NASB.10: For You are great and do wondrous deeds; You alone are God.
NASB.11: Teach me Your way, O LORD; I will walk in Your truth; Unite my heart to fear Your name.
NASB.12: I will give thanks to You, O Lord my God, with all my heart, And will glorify Your name forever.
NASB.13: For Your lovingkindness toward me is great, And You have delivered my soul from the depths of Sheol.
NASB.14: O God, arrogant men have risen up against me, And a band of violent men have sought my life, And they have not set You before them.
NASB.15: But You, O Lord, are a God merciful and gracious, Slow to anger and abundant in lovingkindness and truth.
NASB.16: Turn to me, and be gracious to me; Oh grant Your strength to Your servant, And save the son of Your handmaid.
NASB.17: Show me a sign for good, That those who hate me may see [it] and be ashamed, Because You, O LORD, have helped me and comforted me.

NLT.1:
Bend down, O LORD, and hear my prayer;
answer me, for I need your help.
NLT.2:
Protect me, for I am devoted to you.
Save me, for I serve you and trust you.
You are my God.
NLT.3:
Be merciful to me, O Lord,
for I am calling on you constantly.
NLT.4:
Give me happiness, O Lord,
for I give myself to you.
NLT.5:
O Lord, you are so good, so ready to forgive,
so full of unfailing love for all who ask for your help.
NLT.6:
Listen closely to my prayer, O LORD;
hear my urgent cry.
NLT.7:
I will call to you whenever I’m in trouble,
and you will answer me.
NLT.8:
No pagan god is like you, O Lord.
None can do what you do!
NLT.9:
All the nations you made
will come and bow before you, Lord;
they will praise your holy name.
NLT.10:
For you are great and perform wonderful deeds.
You alone are God.
NLT.11:
Teach me your ways, O LORD,
that I may live according to your truth!
Grant me purity of heart,
so that I may honor you.
NLT.12:
With all my heart I will praise you, O Lord my God.
I will give glory to your name forever,
NLT.13:
for your love for me is very great.
You have rescued me from the depths of death.
NLT.14:
O God, insolent people rise up against me;
a violent gang is trying to kill me.
You mean nothing to them.
NLT.15:
But you, O Lord,
are a God of compassion and mercy,
slow to get angry
and filled with unfailing love and faithfulness.
NLT.16:
Look down and have mercy on me.
Give your strength to your servant;
save me, the son of your servant.
NLT.17:
Send me a sign of your favor.
Then those who hate me will be put to shame,
for you, O LORD, help and comfort me.

WEB.1: A Prayer by David. Hear, Yahweh, and answer me, for I am poor and needy.
WEB.2: Preserve my soul, for I am godly. You, my God, save your servant who trusts in you.
WEB.3: Be merciful to me, Lord, for I call to you all day long.
WEB.4: Bring joy to the soul of your servant, for to you, Lord, do I lift up my soul.
WEB.5: For you, Lord, are good, and ready to forgive; abundant in loving kindness to all those who call on you.
WEB.6: Hear, Yahweh, my prayer. Listen to the voice of my petitions.
WEB.7: In the day of my trouble I will call on you, for you will answer me.
WEB.8: There is no one like you among the gods, Lord, nor any deeds like your deeds.
WEB.9: All nations you have made will come and worship before you, Lord. They shall glorify your name.
WEB.10: For you are great, and do wondrous things. You are God alone.
WEB.11: Teach me your way, Yahweh. I will walk in your truth. Make my heart undivided to fear your name.
WEB.12: I will praise you, Lord my God, with my whole heart. I will glorify your name forever more.
WEB.13: For your loving kindness is great toward me. You have delivered my soul from the lowest Sheol.
WEB.14: God, the proud have risen up against me. A company of violent men have sought after my soul, and they don’t hold regard for you before them.
WEB.15: But you, Lord, are a merciful and gracious God, slow to anger, and abundant in loving kindness and truth.
WEB.16: Turn to me, and have mercy on me! Give your strength to your servant. Save the son of your servant.
WEB.17: Show me a sign of your goodness, that those who hate me may see it, and be shamed, because you, Yahweh, have helped me, and comforted me.

CSB17.1:
A prayer of David. [1] Listen, LORD, and answer me,
for I am poor and needy. ⓘ
CSB17.2:
Protect my life, for I am faithful. ⓙ
You are my God; save your servant who trusts in you. ⓚ
CSB17.3:
Be gracious to me, Lord,
for I call to you all day long. ⓛ
CSB17.4:
Bring joy to your servant’s life,
because I appeal to you, Lord. ⓜ
CSB17.5:
For you, Lord, are kind and ready to forgive, ⓝ
abounding in faithful love to all who call on you. ⓐ
CSB17.6:
LORD, hear my prayer;
listen to my plea for mercy. ⓑ
CSB17.7:
I call on you in the day of my distress,
for you will answer me. ⓒ
CSB17.8:
Lord, there is no one like you among the gods,
and there are no works like yours. ⓓ
CSB17.9:
All the nations you have made
will come and bow down before you, Lord,
and will honor your name. ⓔ
CSB17.10:
For you are great and perform wonders;
you alone are God. ⓕ
CSB17.11:
Teach me your way, LORD,
and I will live by your truth.
Give me an undivided mind to fear your name. ⓖ
CSB17.12:
I will praise you with all my heart, Lord my God,
and will honor your name forever. ⓗ
CSB17.13:
For your faithful love for me is great,
and you rescue my life from the depths of Sheol. ⓘ
CSB17.14:
God, arrogant people have attacked me;
a gang of ruthless men intends to kill me.
They do not let you guide them. [1] ⓙ
CSB17.15:
But you, Lord, are a compassionate and gracious God,
slow to anger and abounding in faithful love and truth. ⓚ
CSB17.16:
Turn to me and be gracious to me.
Give your strength to your servant;
save the son of your female servant. ⓛ
CSB17.17:
Show me a sign of your goodness;
my enemies will see and be put to shame
because you, LORD, have helped and comforted me. ⓜ

AMP.1:
A Prayer of David. Incline Your ear, O LORD, and answer me,
For I am distressed and needy [I long for Your help].
AMP.2:
Protect my life (soul), for I am godly and faithful;
O You my God, save Your servant, who trusts in You [believing in You and relying on You, confidently committing everything to You].
AMP.3:
Be gracious and merciful to me, O Lord,
For to You I cry out all the day long.
AMP.4:
Make Your servant rejoice,
For to You, O Lord, I lift up my soul [all that I am—in prayer].
AMP.5:
For You, O Lord, are good, and ready to forgive [our sins, sending them away, completely letting them go forever and ever];
And abundant in lovingkindness and overflowing in mercy to all those who call upon You.
AMP.6:
Hear, O LORD, my prayer;
And listen attentively to the voice of my supplications (specific requests)!
AMP.7:
In the day of my trouble I will call upon You,
For You will answer me.
AMP.8:
There is no one like You among the gods, O Lord,
Nor are there any works [of wonder and majesty] like Yours.
AMP.9:
All nations whom You have made shall come and kneel down in worship before You, O Lord,
And they shall glorify Your name.
AMP.10:
For You are great and do wondrous works!
You alone are God.
AMP.11:
Teach me Your way, O LORD,
I will walk and live in Your truth;
Direct my heart to fear Your name [with awe-inspired reverence and submissive wonder].
AMP.12:
I will give thanks and praise You, O Lord my God, with all my heart;
And will glorify Your name forevermore.
AMP.13:
For great is Your lovingkindness and graciousness toward me;
And You have rescued my life from the depths of Sheol [from death].
AMP.14:
O God, arrogant and insolent men have risen up against me;
A band of violent men have sought my life,
And they have not set You before them.
AMP.15:
But You, O Lord, are a God [who protects and is] merciful and gracious,
Slow to anger and abounding in lovingkindness and truth.
AMP.16:
Turn to me, and be gracious to me;
Grant Your strength [Your might and the power to resist temptation] to Your servant,
And save the son of Your handmaid.
AMP.17:
Show me a sign of [Your] goodwill,
That those who hate me may see it and be ashamed,
Because You, O LORD, helped and comforted me.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
Bend an ear, God;
answer me. I'm one miserable wretch!
MSG.2:
Keep me safe—haven't I lived a good life?
Help your servant—I'm depending on you!
MSG.3:
You're my God; have mercy on me.
I count on you from morning to night.
MSG.4:
Give your servant a happy life;
I put myself in your hands!
MSG.5:
You're well-known as good and forgiving,
bighearted to all who ask for help.
MSG.6:
Pay attention, God, to my prayer;
bend down and listen to my cry for help.
MSG.7:
Every time I'm in trouble I call on you,
confident that you'll answer.
MSG.8:
There's no one quite like you among the gods, O Lord,
and nothing to compare with your works.
MSG.9:
All the nations you made are on their way,
ready to give honor to you, O Lord,
Ready to put your beauty on display,
MSG.10:
parading your greatness,
And the great things you do—
God, you're the one, there's no one but you!
MSG.11:
Train me, God, to walk straight;
then I'll follow your true path.
Put me together, one heart and mind;
then, undivided, I'll worship in joyful fear.
MSG.12:
From the bottom of my heart I thank you, dear Lord;
I've never kept secret what you're up to.
MSG.13:
You've always been great toward me—what love!
You snatched me from the brink of disaster!
MSG.14:
God, these bullies have reared their heads!
A gang of thugs is after me—
and they don't care a thing about you.
MSG.15:
But you, O God, are both tender and kind,
not easily angered, immense in love,
and you never, never quit.
MSG.16:
So look me in the eye and show kindness,
give your servant the strength to go on,
save your dear, dear child!
MSG.17:
Make a show of how much you love me
so the bullies who hate me will stand there slack-jawed,
As you, God, gently and powerfully
put me back on my feet.

NIV.1:
Psalm 86
A prayer of David.
Hear, O Lord, and answer me,
for I am poor and needy.
NIV.2:
Guard my life, for I am devoted to you.
You are my God; save your servant
who trusts in you.
NIV.3:
Have mercy on me, O Lord,
for I call to you all day long.
NIV.4:
Bring joy to your servant,
for to you, O Lord,
I lift up my soul.
NIV.5:
You are forgiving and good, O Lord,
abounding in love to all who call to you.
NIV.6:
Hear my prayer, O Lord;
listen to my cry for mercy.
NIV.7:
In the day of my trouble I will call to you,
for you will answer me.
NIV.8:
Among the gods there is none like you, O Lord;
no deeds can compare with yours.
NIV.9:
All the nations you have made
will come and worship before you, O Lord;
they will bring glory to your name.
NIV.10:
For you are great and do marvelous deeds;
you alone are God.
NIV.11:
Teach me your way, O Lord,
and I will walk in your truth;
give me an undivided heart,
that I may fear your name.
NIV.12:
I will praise you, O Lord my God, with all my heart;
I will glorify your name forever.
NIV.13:
For great is your love toward me;
you have delivered me from the depths of the grave.
NIV.14:
The arrogant are attacking me, O God;
a band of ruthless men seeks my life—
men without regard for you.
NIV.15:
But you, O Lord, are a compassionate and gracious God,
slow to anger, abounding in love and faithfulness.
NIV.16:
Turn to me and have mercy on me;
grant your strength to your servant
and save the son of your maidservant.
NIV.17:
Give me a sign of your goodness,
that my enemies may see it and be put to shame,
for you, O Lord, have helped me and comforted me.

NRSVCE.1:

for I am poor and needy.

NRSVCE.3:

NRSVCE.5:
For you, O Lord, are good and forgiving,
abounding in steadfast love to all who call on you.

Give ear, O LORD, to my prayer;

In the day of my trouble I call on you,

There is none like you among the gods, O Lord,

NRSVCE.9:

NRSVCE.10:
For you are great and do wondrous things;

because you, LORD, have helped me and comforted me.

NET.1: Listen O LORD! Answer me! For I am oppressed and needy.
NET.2: Protect me, for I am loyal! O my God, deliver your servant, who trusts in you!
NET.3: Have mercy on me, O Lord, for I cry out to you all day long!
NET.4: Make your servant glad, for to you, O Lord, I pray!
NET.5: Certainly O Lord, you are kind and forgiving, and show great faithfulness to all who cry out to you.
NET.6: O LORD, hear my prayer! Pay attention to my plea for mercy!
NET.7: In my time of trouble I cry out to you, for you will answer me.
NET.8: None can compare to you among the gods, O Lord! Your exploits are incomparable!
NET.9: All the nations, whom you created, will come and worship you, O Lord. They will honor your name.
NET.10: For you are great and do amazing things. You alone are God.
NET.11: O LORD, teach me how you want me to live! Then I will obey your commands. Make me wholeheartedly committed to you!
NET.12: O Lord, my God, I will give you thanks with my whole heart! I will honor your name continually!
NET.13: For you will extend your great loyal love to me, and will deliver my life from the depths of Sheol.
NET.14: O God, arrogant men attack me; a gang of ruthless men, who do not respect you, seek my life.
NET.15: But you, O Lord, are a compassionate and merciful God. You are patient and demonstrate great loyal love and faithfulness.
NET.16: Turn toward me and have mercy on me! Give your servant your strength! Deliver your slave!
NET.17: Show me evidence of your favor! Then those who hate me will see it and be ashamed, for you, O LORD, will help me and comfort me.
